findings.claim: ggpaintr has TWO parallel data-source widget/resolution implementations — the single-instance/formula-local path (`ptr_setup_source_uis` in paintr-server.R) and the multi-instance shared-coordinator HOST path (`ptr_setup_panel_sources` in paintr-shared-ui.R) — and a fix applied to one does not propagate to the other.
findings.detail: This is the root structural reason ADR-0025-area fixes (#7 fileInput reset, source_file_reset flag, file_reset gate) landed only single-instance and had to be separately ported. The host path mirrors the single path's renderUI/registry-dispatch shape but is a distinct code path with its own renderUI, its own resolve_upload_source call, and its own mutex.
findings.verification: Both symbols present at HEAD 29571ac: ptr_setup_source_uis (R/paintr-server.R:2306, wired in ptr_server_internal at :857) and ptr_setup_panel_sources (R/paintr-shared-ui.R:513). Each is a distinct function with its own renderUI scope and its own resolve_upload_source call (shared-ui.R:673), confirming the two-parallel-paths claim. Only line numbers drifted.

findings.claim: At coordinator HOST scope ptr_bind_shared_consumer_uis is called with state=NULL (R/paintr-server.R:2955, ADR-0006), so the if(!is.null(state)) block — including both req(state$bound_names[[id]]()) gates — never runs; host scope instead runs its own is.null(state) branches: a synthetic host_env extending eval_env with the panel-resolved df (server.R:3115-3164) and a per-picker double-render clear-hold latch (server.R:3297-3313), driving the picker off panel_sources[[sid]]() reactives.
findings.detail: Consequence: the single-instance suppress-on-vacate gate is structurally absent at host scope. The same binder runs at per-instance embedded scope WITH a real state, so behaviour diverges by call site, not by code. Reached via ptr_bind_local_shared_consumers (server.R:3269) from both the single-instance/embedded server (state passed) and the coordinator host (state omitted).
findings.source: R/paintr-server.R:2891-3256 (ptr_bind_shared_consumer_uis), :3095-3156 (if(!is.null(state)) block); R/paintr-shared-ui.R:386 (host call omits state=)
findings.verification: ptr_bind_shared_consumer_uis (server.R:2972) declares state=NULL as default. Host reaches it via ptr_bind_local_shared_consumers (shared-ui.R:414), which omits state= -> NULL at host scope. is.null(state) branches confirmed: synthetic host_env extending eval_env with panel df (server.R:3139-3187) and the per-picker cleared_for_identity/held_clear clear-hold latch (server.R:3030-3335); single-instance passes a real state. Behaviour diverges by call site, not code. Line numbers drifted.

findings.claim: The downstream consumer's new-source-clear is edge-triggered on identity sid#datapath#boundName (server.R:2621); single-instance flips boundName via state$bound_names so both upload beats re-clear, while at host scope state=NULL forces boundName="" so identity stabilises after beat 1 — but a host-scope latch (cleared_for_identity armed on the clear edge + held_clear re-assertion, server.R:3297-3311) now carries the clear across the trailing render, so the stale current no longer rides back (the beat-2 revert was fixed).
findings.detail: Static mechanism confirmed in source: consumer_upstream_source_state builds identity = paste0(sid,'#',dp,'#',bn) joined by '||', edge-triggered in consumer_clear_for_new_source via `src_seen && !identical(identity,last_identity) && user_supplied`. The sid→layer_name bound_names fallback (server.R:2576-2579) means a NULL state yields bn="" permanently. The TWO-beat runtime divergence (single clears, host reverts) was AppDriver-observed in-session (dev/scratch/ppupload7-probes/) and NOT re-run this pass; the static code path is fully consistent with it. Refutes an earlier 'req-gate serialization' theory.
findings.verification: Confirmed in R/paintr-server.R: consumer reads state$bound_names[[sid]] (line 2604) with a sid->layer_name fallback (lines 2618-2619), and the host-scope comment 'bn always "" -- no state$bound_names to read' is present at line 2629, matching the claim that a NULL state forces boundName="" so identity stabilises. boot_seed_selected (line 2504) and consumer_seed_decision (line 2508) exist; the seed_landed gate is at lines 2557-2563. The clear-edge identity mechanism (sid#datapath#boundName) and the host-scope latch are present in source; the runtime two-beat behaviour was probe-observed in-session (not re-run) as the finding itself notes. Static code path consistent with the claim. Line numbers drifted.

findings.claim: Porting #7's source_file_reset flag to the host path is simpler than the single-instance original because in `ptr_setup_panel_sources` the renderUI, the shortcut, the flag, and the `resolve_upload_source` call all live in ONE `local({...})` scope — so the flag is a local `reactiveVal` read directly at the resolve call, with no cross-function plumbing, unlike single-instance which must publish on `state$source_file_reset` and read it back in the per-instance pipeline binders.
findings.detail: The planned host edits are additive in that one scope; resolve_upload_source already accepts `file_reset` (server.R:1251, `if (isTRUE(file_reset)) file_info <- NULL` at :1287). The host path uses a synthetic host_state list (eval_env + throwaway bound_names env + resolve_errors) at shared-ui.R:593-597 and returns reactive(slot()). Single-instance plumbing confirmed: file_reset_rv published on state$source_file_reset (server.R:2329-2330) and read back at :1536/:1614. Plan: dev/plans/0025-item7-followup-host-shared-consumer-clear/plan.html (exists).
findings.verification: ptr_setup_panel_sources in R/paintr-shared-ui.R builds its own bound_names env and host_state list and calls resolve_upload_source within one local scope (~654-666), confirming the 'one local({}) scope' simplicity claim. resolve_upload_source exists in R/paintr-server.R and accepts a file_reset arg; state$source_file_reset is an env keyed by SOURCE id created/driven by ptr_setup_source_uis (state-init comment at lines 310-322). The single-instance plumbing publishing on state$source_file_reset is documented in that same init block. The referenced plan dir under dev/plans/ is a project artifact, not source. Claim's structural assertions hold; line numbers drifted.

findings.claim: A single-instance `ppVar()` consumer picker does NOT bind at boot — it stays absent from `input` until BOTH its upstream source has data (the `req(state$bound_names[[id]]())` gate clears) AND the layer's Controls subtab is opened; so a probe/test must provide the source first, then set `<layer>_subtab="Controls"`, then read the picker.
findings.detail: Single-instance source id pattern observed: `ggplot_0_ppUpload_NA`, shortcut `ggplot_0_ppUpload_NA_shortcut`; consumer picker id `ggplot_1_1_ppVar_NA` appeared only after typing an env source AND opening the subtab. Static mechanism (ptr_setup_consumer_uis suspend + req gate) confirmed; the specific runtime ids are probe-observed (discover.R is a thin 553-byte discovery script, not re-run this pass). Corroborated by project rule .claude/rules/testing.md (consumer var pickers are suspended under a renderUI subtab).
findings.verification: ptr_setup_consumer_uis (server.R:2682) contains the source-ready gate shiny::req(state$bound_names[[source_ready$id]]()) at :2842; renderUI-under-subtab suspension corroborated by .claude/rules/testing.md:68-71 and :533-534 ('var pickers ... suspended ... under the layer Data subtab'). Picker stays absent until source has data AND Controls subtab opened. Runtime widget-id specifics remain probe-observed per the finding's own note. Line numbers drifted.

findings.claim: A consumer-clear regression test MUST select a column present in BOTH the pre- and post-switch datasets (e.g. `body_mass_g`), because a `selectInput`/`selectpicker` silently drops a selection that is no longer a valid option — so a test using a column unique to the old dataset passes even when the clear logic is broken (false green).
findings.detail: This trap masked the bug during static analysis: it looks 'cleared' merely because the option vanished, not because our clear fired. The probe deliberately used body_mass_g (present in both typed_ds and penguins.csv) to expose the persistence.
findings.verification: Durable test-design fact (shinyWidgets selectInput/pickerInput silently drops a selection that is not a current option), so a consumer-clear test must select a column present in BOTH datasets. Probe README still present at dev/scratch/ppupload7-probes/README.md.

findings.claim: To observe internal reactive state inside a shinytest2 child process, wrap the target ggpaintr internal in the fixture `app.R` via `assignInNamespace("fn", wrapper, ns="ggpaintr")` after `pkgload::load_all`, having the wrapper `message()` its state; `AppDriver$get_logs()` then captures it as stderr lines — parent-process `trace()` is invisible to the child.
findings.detail: Used this session to log consumer_upstream_source_state identity + consumer_clear_for_new_source verdict, producing the differential single-vs-host evidence. Capture the original with `ggpaintr:::fn` BEFORE reassigning. Parse via as.data.frame(app$get_logs())$message grep 'PROBE_'.
findings.verification: Confirmed the technique matches the project rule (.claude/rules/serena-tools.md, 2 assignInNamespace mentions): wrap a ggpaintr internal in the fixture app.R via assignInNamespace(fn,wrapper,ns='ggpaintr') after pkgload::load_all and message() state; parent-process trace() is invisible to the shinytest2 child.

findings.claim: The fileInput filename "pill" is rendered by the browser's native file widget and is NOT observable via chromote/CDP (`get_html`/`get_value`), so any success criterion about the pill clearing must be verified by a human EYEBALL in a real browser, never by an AppDriver assertion.
findings.detail: This is why #7 and its host-path follow-up carry a manual 'Gate 0' eyeball; the flag/vacate/consumer-clear pieces ARE harness-observable and carry the automated regression. A green shinytest2 run does not prove the pill cleared.
findings.verification: Durable harness fact: the fileInput filename pill is rendered by the browser's native file widget and is NOT observable via chromote/CDP get_html/get_value, so any pill-clear success criterion must be a human eyeball, never an AppDriver assertion. (Applies to both stamped instances.)

findings.claim: `/exec-plan <plan-dir>` only consumes a `/decision-to-plan` output directory — it reads `<plan-dir>/index.md` (merge order + parallel groups + worked-example matrix) and per-plan `NN-<slug>.html` files with HTML-comment frontmatter (`number`/`slug`/`depends_on`/`parallel_group`) + a `Files this plan owns:` block + a mandatory worked-example table, and REFUSES TO START if index.md or the worked-example section is missing.
findings.detail: A hand-authored single plan.html blessed only by /implementable is NOT exec-plan-ready (lacks index.md, frontmatter, NN-slug filename, worked-example table). exec-plan also runs autonomously, so a harness-blind eyeball success criterion cannot be grounded by it.
findings.verification: Confirmed against ~/.claude/skills/exec-plan/SKILL.md: requires <plan-dir>/index.md (merge order + parallel groups + Worked-example matrix) + per-plan NN-<slug>.html with HTML-comment frontmatter (number/slug/depends_on/parallel_group) + 'Files this plan owns:' + Worked-example section; refuses to start if a plan is PASS by neither stamp nor index table, or if the worked-example section is missing.
